disclaimer.text: This software and the documents it generates are for general informational purposes only. The Corporation is not a law firm and does not provide legal, tax, or accounting advice. Use of this document generator does not create an attorney-client or other professional relationship. Generated documents are not a substitute for advice from a licensed attorney, tax advisor, or accountant. You should consult qualified professionals before relying on any generated documents. Documents have not been prepared with your specific circumstances in mind and may not be suitable for your business. Default values and configurations do not constitute recommendations. Documents are provided "AS IS" without warranties of any kind, express or implied, including warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, title, or noninfringement. By using these documents, you assume all risk and liability. Review all generated documents carefully for accuracy before use. Look for any placeholder text (TBD, YYYY-MM-DD, bracketed text) requiring your attention and modification. This system generates governance documents for AI-operated entities. The legal landscape for AI agent authority is evolving. You should consult with counsel familiar with your jurisdiction's treatment of algorithmic management and AI agent authority.

corporation.governing_statute_full: Delaware General Corporation Law (Title 8, Delaware Code)

llc.governing_statute_full: Wyoming Limited Liability Company Act (W.S. § 17-29-101 et seq.)

categories.label: Recurring obligations (rent, subscriptions, utilities, payroll within approved budget)
Vendor payments against approved purchase orders
Professional services (legal, accounting) against approved retainers
Government filing fees and compliance costs

authority_precedence.label: Applicable law (DGCL, Wyoming LLC Act, federal law, regulations)
Charter documents (Certificate of Incorporation / Articles of Organization)
Principal / CEO directives (specific instructions to the Agent)
This Delegation Schedule (default autonomy lanes and limits)
Agent heuristics and judgment (lowest priority -- always overridden by anything above)

escalation.reason: Agreement template modified in restricted areas

checks.message: Exclusivity clause not permitted in SaaS lane
IP assignment clause not permitted in SaaS lane
Exclusivity, non-compete, or equity compensation not permitted for contractor engagement
Rate increase exceeds 5% limit for renewal lane
Price increase exceeds 10% limit for renewal lane
Admin update lane does not allow changes to economics, scope, or legal terms
Premium increase exceeds 15% limit for insurance lane

conditions.label: No modifications to indemnification or governing law
Annual cost within Tier 1 vendor limit
No volume commitments beyond current quarter
No IP assignment beyond work product

autonomy_lanes.label: Contractor renewal at same or lower rate
Address or administrative updates on existing agreements

conditions.label: Amount matches filed or estimated return

auto_suspension_triggers.label: Audit log failure
auto_suspension_triggers.description: Unable to write to append-only log, or gap/inconsistency detected

auto_suspension_triggers.label: Credential anomaly
auto_suspension_triggers.description: Unauthorized access, credential compromise, or use outside authorized scope

auto_suspension_triggers.label: Conflicting instructions
auto_suspension_triggers.description: Multiple authorized sources contradict each other, unresolvable by precedence rules

auto_suspension_triggers.label: Policy-evidence mismatch
auto_suspension_triggers.description: Tier 2 action appears executed without valid approval artifact

auto_suspension_triggers.label: Unauthorized scope expansion
auto_suspension_triggers.description: Agent authority modified outside governance process

auto_suspension_triggers.label: External breach notification
auto_suspension_triggers.description: Data breach, security incident, or legal hold notice received

retention_schedule.label: Financial records and tax filings
retention_schedule.governing_requirement: IRS requirements; state law where longer

change_control_rules.label: Major version update (new capabilities, changed behavior)
Change of Agent provider or underlying model
Change that alters delegation scope, tier behavior, or self-governance constraints

documents.title: Agent Delegation Schedule
documents.preamble: This is the controlling operational artifact for Agent Operator authority. It is adopted by reference in the Operating Agreement (LLC) and Bylaws (Corporation). The Principal/CEO may amend this schedule within the adjustment envelope (Section 9.1) without Board/Member action. Any amendment that expands delegated authority requires Board/Member resolution. The Agent Operator may not amend this schedule.

metadata_fields.label: Adopted by
metadata_fields.placeholder: TBD (Initial Board Consent / Initial Member Consent)

content.text: When instructions or rules conflict, the following precedence applies (highest to lowest):

When the Agent detects a conflict between levels, it must halt, record the conflict, and escalate to the Principal. The Agent must not resolve ambiguity by choosing the interpretation that expands its own authority.

The Agent may not commit more than **{{spending_defaults.per_vendor_annual_cap}}** in aggregate annual spending to any single vendor without Tier 2 approval, even if individual transactions are within Tier 1 limits.
Any expenditure not covered by a category above, or exceeding any limit above, requires Tier 2 approval before commitment.
Autonomy lanes define categories of action the Agent may take without per-instance approval, provided all lane conditions are met. If any condition is not met, the action escalates to Tier 2.
**Not Tier 1 (always Tier 2 or higher):** W-2 employee hiring, exclusive engagements, IP assignment beyond standard work-for-hire, non-compete obligations, equity compensation.
**Not Tier 1:** Opening/closing accounts, changing signers, setting up new payment methods, wire transfers to new recipients.
A valid Tier 2 approval requires all of the following:
Every Tier 2 approval must be recorded with:

lines: approver: [name and role of approving person]
approver_auth_method: [platform login | email with verified sender | signed document | board resolution]
scope: [specific description of what is approved]
conditions: [any conditions attached to the approval, or "none"]
channel: [platform approval / email / signed document / board resolution]
expiry: [date after which approval lapses if unexecuted]

content.text: For defensibility-first operation, negative consent is disabled by default and no-response is never approval.
Negative consent may only be enabled by explicit Board/Member resolution that identifies:

items: The exact action categories permitted for negative consent.
A per-action dollar cap and aggregate cap.
A minimum response window of 24 hours.
A mandatory expiration date for the authorization window.
Additional monitoring and reporting requirements.

content.text: **Negative consent is never available for:** equity actions, litigation, governance amendments, state/federal filings requiring natural-person attestation, actions above **$5,000**, or any Tier 3 action.

items: The Principal holds root/admin credentials for all financial and legal accounts.
The Agent receives scoped credentials provisioned by the Principal.
The Agent may not create new accounts or credentials without Tier 2 approval.
The Agent may not share, export, or store credentials outside the entity's authorized systems.

Credential rotation: the Agent must flag expiring credentials at least 14 days before expiry. The Principal rotates root credentials; the Agent may rotate its own scoped credentials within the same scope.
Only the Principal may change modes manually (except automatic triggers).
The Agent must prominently display the current mode in every action record and report.
Mode changes are logged as governance events in the audit trail.
During Principal Unavailable mode, time-critical legal deadlines (filing deadlines, tax payments against approved returns) remain Tier 1 if they are ministerial and previously approved.

content.text: The Agent Operator must immediately enter Incident Lockdown mode if any of the following occur:

items: The Agent must immediately notify the Principal through all established channels.
The Agent must preserve the current state and all evidence of the triggering event.
The Agent must not take corrective action without Principal authorization.
Only the Principal may lift the lockdown after reviewing the trigger.

content.text: For any auto-suspension trigger or operational anomaly, the Agent must produce a structured incident report within 1 hour, per the schema defined in the Signing and Records Standard.
The CEO (Corporation) or Principal (LLC) may modify this Delegation Schedule without Board/Member action **only within the following envelope**:
All adjustments must be recorded in the amendment history (Section 12) with the date, description, and authorizing person.
9.2 Structural changes requiring governance document amendment
The following changes are outside the scope of this Delegation Schedule and require amendment of the Bylaws (Corporation) or Operating Agreement (LLC):

items: Adding or removing Authority Tiers
Changing the definition or scope of Tier 3 (non-delegable) actions
Modifying the Agent's legal status or designation
Altering the safe harbor or its exclusions
Changing the precedence hierarchy
Modifying self-governance constraints
The Agent provider may not use entity data for training, benchmarking, or any secondary purpose without explicit Principal written consent.
Entity data processed by the Agent must remain within systems authorized by the Principal.
The Agent must flag any provider terms that grant data usage rights and escalate as Tier 2.
Upon Agent replacement, the provider must delete or return all entity data within 30 days per the service agreement.

content.text: This Delegation Schedule must be reviewed and re-authorized by the Principal/CEO/Board at least annually. If not re-authorized within 30 days of the review date:

items: The schedule remains in effect but all Tier 1 spending limits are reduced to 50% of stated values.
The Agent must escalate the re-authorization as a pending Tier 2 action.
After 90 days without re-authorization, all Tier 1 autonomous authority is suspended and only Tier 1 record-keeping and reporting continue.

documents.title: Assumptions and Decision Log
documents.preamble: Use this decision log to capture the governing factual and legal assumptions applied in the formation set and the entity's operating model.

items: Entity type: Wyoming Limited Liability Company
Formation state: **Wyoming**
Governing statute: Wyoming Limited Liability Company Act (W.S. § 17-29-101 et seq.)
Legislative context: Wyoming DAO LLC Supplement (W.S. § 17-31-101 et seq.)
Filing authority: Wyoming Secretary of State
Principal business address: `{{company_address}}`
Registered agent (must have Wyoming address): `{{registered_agent_name}}`
Freedom of contract basis: W.S. § 17-29-110
Entity type: Delaware C-Corporation
Formation state: **Delaware**
Governing statute: Delaware General Corporation Law (DGCL, Title 8, Delaware Code)
Filing authority: Delaware Division of Corporations
Principal business address: `{{company_address}}`
Registered agent (must have Delaware address): `{{registered_agent_name}}`
Board delegation basis: DGCL § 141(a)
Director liability elimination: DGCL § 102(b)(7)
Indemnification: DGCL § 145
Founders / owners and legal names: `{{founders_list}}`
Ownership allocation: `{{founders_table}}`
Founder vesting baseline: four-year vesting with a one-year cliff unless modified by definitive issuance documents.
Transfer restrictions baseline: governing document transfer restrictions, rights of first refusal, and approval requirements apply unless expressly waived in writing.
EIN status: the Company will obtain and record an EIN promptly following formation and before payroll, tax filings, or account openings requiring an EIN.
Tax classification election (if LLC): default statutory treatment applies unless changed by a filed election approved by the appropriate authority.
Fiscal year end: `{{fiscal_year_end}}`
Accounting method baseline: cash method unless the Company later adopts another method in compliance with tax and accounting requirements.
LLC management model: **Manager-managed** (required for the agent-operated structure).
Corporation board size and initial directors: `{{board_size}}` directors: `{{directors_list}}`
Officer roles and appointees: `{{officers_list}}`
Signature authority matrix: see the Agent Delegation Schedule and the applicable initial written consent or board consent.

content.text: Define how governance documents are approved, signed, and archived, including standards for AI agent execution, audit integrity, and incident response.

items: Draft or edit document in Markdown.
Open pull request with summary of legal/business deltas.
Obtain approval from authorized decision-makers per the Authority Tier framework.
Mark document version as "approved for execution".
Agent drafts document and classifies the action by Authority Tier.
**Tier 1**: Agent logs the action, executes, and records the document with an agent execution block.
**Tier 2**: Agent creates a proposal record. Action is queued until a valid approval artifact is recorded. Silence is not approval.
**Tier 3**: Agent may prepare the draft but must not execute. Routed to the Principal.
Preferred format: e-signature packet generated from final Markdown conversion.
Signature blocks must include printed name, title/capacity, and date.
Use UTC date format in ISO style: `YYYY-MM-DD`.

**Natural-person attestation rule.** Where any statute, regulation, government portal, financial institution, or counterparty requires a natural person's signature, only a designated human may sign or attest. The Agent must not sign, attest, or produce any block that could be mistaken for a human signature.

content.text: When the Agent Operator executes an action, the internal audit record must include:

lines: policy_rule_id: [specific autonomy lane ID or spending category]
action_hash: [SHA-256 hash of the action content]
previous_entry_hash: [SHA-256 hash of the prior audit entry]
evidence_uri: [reference to source documents or approval artifacts]

items: Each audit entry includes the SHA-256 hash of the previous entry, creating an append-only hash chain.
The first entry uses a genesis hash derived from the entity's formation date and EIN.
**Weekly checkpoint**: compute and store a checkpoint hash in a separate immutable location.
**Quarterly verification**: verify the full hash chain from genesis to current.

content.text: For Critical and High severity incidents, the Agent must produce a structured report within 1 hour containing:

lines: containment_actions: [actions taken or "none -- awaiting Principal direction"]

content.text: Upon Agent Operator replacement or termination:

items: Transfer all records, credentials, pending proposals, and audit logs within **7 days**.
Provide a complete, verified data export in portable formats.
Delete all entity data within **30 days** and certify deletion in writing.
Transfer the hash chain intact with verification.
Pending Tier 2 proposals transfer as-is; approvals do not transfer.

documents.title: Agent Operator Service Agreement
documents.preamble: Service agreement governing the provider's operation of the Agent system and related services for the entity.

content.text: This Agent Operator Service Agreement (this "Agreement") is entered into as of `{{effective_date}}` by and between **{{entity_legal_name}}** (the "Entity") and `{{provider_legal_name}}` (the "Provider").

Provider will supply the Agent Operator platform and related support services used by the Entity to operate within the authority granted by the Entity's governing documents, internal controls, and written instructions. Provider will perform the services in a professional and workmanlike manner, using personnel with appropriate skill and care.

items: Provider acknowledges that the Agent Operator has only the authority expressly delegated by the Entity and may not expand or reconfigure that authority except pursuant to written instructions from an authorized representative of the Entity.
Provider will maintain technical and administrative controls sufficient to support immediate suspension, restriction, or revocation of Agent operations upon the Entity's request.

Provider may not use Entity data for training, benchmarking, model improvement, analytics, or any other secondary purpose except to the extent expressly authorized in a separate written agreement signed by the Entity.

content.text: 3. Security, Incident Response, and Audit Rights

items: Provider will maintain commercially reasonable administrative, technical, and physical safeguards, including role-based access controls, encryption in transit and at rest where commercially appropriate, audit logging, credential rotation support, and incident detection and response procedures.

Provider will notify the Entity without undue delay and, in any event, within twenty-four (24) hours after discovering any security incident affecting Entity data or systems, and will provide updates regarding containment, remediation, and impact as reasonably requested.

Provider will maintain records reasonably sufficient to demonstrate compliance with this Agreement and will cooperate with the Entity's reasonable audit, diligence, or compliance review requests, subject to appropriate confidentiality protections.

Provider will use commercially reasonable efforts to maintain monthly service availability of at least 99.5%, excluding scheduled maintenance windows disclosed at least forty-eight (48) hours in advance.
Provider will provide commercially reasonable support for Severity 1 incidents within one (1) hour after notice and for other support requests within Provider's standard support windows.

Provider will give the Entity reasonable advance notice of material product, model, hosting, or control changes that could affect delegation scope, auditability, security posture, or operational continuity, and will not implement changes that materially expand Agent authority without the Entity's prior written approval.

content.text: 5. Data Return, Deletion, Liability, and Termination

items: Upon termination or replacement of the Provider, Provider will return Entity data and material operational artifacts in a commercially usable format within seven (7) days and, within thirty (30) days thereafter, delete remaining Entity data from Provider-controlled systems except to the extent retention is required by law, with written deletion certification upon request.

Provider will remain liable for damages arising from unauthorized data use, breach of confidentiality, failure to comply with deletion obligations, and security incidents caused by Provider's breach of this Agreement or failure to maintain commercially reasonable safeguards. Any limitation of liability for ordinary operational errors will not apply to fraud, willful misconduct, gross negligence, or unauthorized data use, and in all other cases will be the greater of fees paid or payable under this Agreement during the prior twelve (12) months or $250,000.

The initial term of this Agreement is one (1) year and will automatically renew for successive one-year periods unless either party gives at least thirty (30) days' prior written notice of non-renewal. The Entity may terminate this Agreement for convenience on thirty (30) days' prior written notice and may terminate immediately for material breach, material security incident, unauthorized data use, insolvency, or repeated service-level failure.

content.text: This Agreement is governed by the laws of `{{jurisdiction}}`, without regard to conflicts of laws principles. The parties consent to the exclusive jurisdiction of the state and federal courts customarily available in that jurisdiction for disputes not otherwise resolved by written agreement. This Agreement may be executed in counterparts and by electronic signature.

documents.title: Agent Operator Service Agreement Checklist
documents.preamble: Minimum required terms for the service agreement between the entity and the Agent Operator provider. This checklist is a governance tool, not a contract.

content.text: Use this checklist to confirm that the executed Agent Operator service agreement includes the minimum governance, security, audit, and data-handling protections required by the Entity.

rows: Provider may not use Entity data for training, benchmarking, or secondary purposes without explicit written Entity consent.
Entity data must remain within systems and jurisdictions authorized by the Entity.
Provider must maintain commercially reasonable confidentiality, access control, and retention protections.
Provider must notify the Entity of any security incident affecting Entity data within 24 hours of discovery.
Provider returns Entity data and operational artifacts in portable form within 7 days after termination or replacement.
Provider deletes remaining Entity data within 30 days, subject only to legally required retention.
Provider supplies written deletion certification on request.
Provider supports scoped credential provisioning, rotation, and revocation.
Provider maintains audit logs of access to Entity data and systems.
Provider supports immediate suspension of Agent operations upon Entity request.
Provider maintains a recognized security controls program (for example, SOC 2 Type II or equivalent).
Entity has reasonable audit or diligence rights regarding Provider compliance.
Provider liability carve-outs cover fraud, willful misconduct, gross negligence, and unauthorized data use.
Agreement includes a reasonable liability cap for ordinary operational errors only.
Provider must provide advance notice of material system or model changes affecting controls, security, or delegation scope.
Entity may terminate for convenience on 30 days notice and immediately for material breach or material security incident.
Post-termination data return, deletion, confidentiality, and cooperation obligations survive termination.
Governing law aligns with the Entity's governing documents or another expressly approved jurisdiction.

documents.title: Certificate of Incorporation
documents.preamble: Filed with the Delaware Secretary of State, Division of Corporations. This document establishes the corporation under the Delaware General Corporation Law.

content.text: The name of the corporation is **{{legal_name}}** (the "Corporation").
The address of the registered office of the Corporation in the State of Delaware is `{{registered_agent_address}}`. The name of the registered agent at such address is `{{registered_agent_name}}`.
The purpose of the Corporation is to engage in any lawful act or activity for which corporations may be organized under the General Corporation Law of Delaware.
The total number of shares of stock which the Corporation shall have authority to issue is `{{authorized_shares}}` shares of Common Stock, par value $`{{par_value}}` per share.

The name and mailing address of the incorporator are:

Name: `{{incorporator_name}}`
Address: `{{incorporator_address}}`

The business and affairs of the Corporation shall be managed by or under the direction of the Board of Directors. The number of directors shall be fixed from time to time by resolution of the Board of Directors. The initial number of directors is `{{board_size}}`.
Elections of directors need not be by written ballot unless the Bylaws of the Corporation shall so provide.

To the fullest extent permitted by the Delaware General Corporation Law, as it now exists or as it may hereafter be amended, a director of the Corporation shall not be personally liable to the Corporation or its stockholders for monetary damages for breach of fiduciary duty as a director.

The Corporation shall indemnify, to the fullest extent permitted by applicable law, any director or officer of the Corporation who was or is a party or is threatened to be made a party to any threatened, pending or completed action, suit or proceeding by reason of the fact that he or she is or was a director or officer of the Corporation.

The Corporation reserves the right to amend, alter, change or repeal any provision contained in this Certificate of Incorporation, in the manner now or hereafter prescribed by statute, and all rights conferred upon stockholders herein are granted subject to this reservation.

The Board of Directors is authorized to designate one or more Agent Operators (as defined in the Bylaws) to act on behalf of the Corporation within the scope of authority delegated by the Board through the Agent Delegation Schedule adopted by reference in the Bylaws. The designation, scope, and constraints of Agent Operator authority shall be governed by the Bylaws and the Agent Delegation Schedule.

documents.title: Bylaws
documents.preamble: Bylaws of {{legal_name}}, a Delaware corporation.

content.text: **Section 1.1 Registered Office.** The registered office of the Corporation shall be at `{{registered_agent_address}}`, and the name of the registered agent at such address is `{{registered_agent_name}}`.

**Section 1.2 Other Offices.** The Corporation may also have offices at such other places as the Board of Directors may from time to time determine. The principal business office of the Corporation shall be at `{{company_address}}`.
**Section 2.1 Annual Meeting.** The annual meeting of stockholders shall be held at such date, time and place as shall be designated by the Board of Directors.
**Section 2.2 Special Meetings.** Special meetings of stockholders may be called by the Board of Directors, the Chairperson of the Board, or the Chief Executive Officer.

**Section 2.3 Notice of Meetings.** Written notice of each meeting of stockholders shall be given not less than ten (10) nor more than sixty (60) days before the date of the meeting to each stockholder entitled to vote at such meeting.

**Section 2.4 Quorum.** The holders of a majority of the shares of stock entitled to vote, present in person or represented by proxy, shall constitute a quorum for the transaction of business at all meetings of the stockholders.

**Section 2.5 Action Without Meeting.** Any action required or permitted to be taken at any annual or special meeting of stockholders may be taken without a meeting, without prior notice and without a vote, if a consent in writing, setting forth the action so taken, shall be signed by the holders of outstanding stock having not less than the minimum number of votes that would be necessary to authorize such action at a meeting.
**Section 3.1 General Powers.** The business and affairs of the Corporation shall be managed by or under the direction of the Board of Directors.
**Section 3.2 Number and Qualification.** The Board of Directors shall consist of `{{board_size}}` director(s). Directors need not be stockholders.

**Section 3.3 Election and Term.** Directors shall be elected at each annual meeting of the stockholders. Each director shall hold office until such director's successor is elected and qualified or until such director's earlier resignation, removal or death.
**Section 3.4 Quorum.** A majority of the total number of directors shall constitute a quorum for the transaction of business.

**Section 3.5 Action Without Meeting.** Any action required or permitted to be taken at any meeting of the Board of Directors or of any committee thereof may be taken without a meeting if all members of the Board or committee consent thereto in writing.
**Section 3.6 Vacancies.** Any vacancy on the Board of Directors may be filled by a majority of the remaining directors, even if less than a quorum.

**Section 4.1 Officers.** The officers of the Corporation shall include a Chief Executive Officer, a Secretary, and a Chief Financial Officer. The Board of Directors may also appoint such other officers as it deems necessary.

**Section 4.2 Election and Term.** Officers shall be elected by the Board of Directors and shall hold office until their successors are elected and qualified or until their earlier resignation, removal or death.

**Section 4.3 Chief Executive Officer.** The Chief Executive Officer shall be the principal executive officer of the Corporation and shall have general supervision of the business and affairs of the Corporation, subject to the direction of the Board of Directors.

**Section 4.4 Secretary.** The Secretary shall keep the minutes of the meetings of the stockholders and the Board of Directors, shall give notice of meetings, and shall have custody of the corporate seal.

**Section 4.5 Chief Financial Officer.** The Chief Financial Officer shall be responsible for the financial affairs of the Corporation, including maintaining the books of account and financial records.

**Section 5.1 Designation.** The Board of Directors may designate one or more Agent Operators to manage the day-to-day operations of the Corporation within the scope of authority set forth in the Agent Delegation Schedule.

**Section 5.2 Agent Delegation Schedule.** The Agent Delegation Schedule, as adopted and amended by the Board, is incorporated by reference and defines the authority tiers, spending limits, autonomy lanes, approval mechanics, and constraints governing the Agent Operator.

**Section 5.3 Authority Limitations.** The Agent Operator may act only within the scope of authority expressly delegated. The Board or CEO may suspend or revoke Agent authority at any time without notice or cause.

**Section 5.4 Safe Harbor.** A director shall not be liable for any action taken by an Agent Operator that was within the scope of the delegated authority as set forth in the Agent Delegation Schedule, provided the Board acted in good faith in adopting and maintaining the Delegation Schedule. This safe harbor does not apply to fraud, willful misconduct, or gross negligence.
**Section 6.1 Certificates.** Shares of stock may be certificated or uncertificated, as determined by the Board of Directors.

**Section 6.2 Transfer Restrictions.** No stockholder shall transfer, sell, assign, pledge, hypothecate, or otherwise dispose of any shares of stock of the Corporation without the prior written consent of the Board of Directors, unless the Board determines otherwise.

**Section 6.3 Right of First Refusal.** The Corporation shall have a right of first refusal with respect to any proposed transfer of shares. Before any stockholder may transfer shares, the stockholder shall first offer such shares to the Corporation on the same terms and conditions as the proposed transfer.

**Section 7.1 Right to Indemnification.** The Corporation shall indemnify each person who was or is a party or is threatened to be made a party to any action, suit or proceeding by reason of the fact that he or she is or was a director, officer, or Agent Operator of the Corporation, to the fullest extent authorized by the Delaware General Corporation Law.
The fiscal year of the Corporation shall end on `{{fiscal_year_end}}`.
These Bylaws may be amended or repealed by the Board of Directors or by the stockholders.

documents.title: Action by Written Consent of the Sole Incorporator

content.text: The undersigned, being the sole incorporator of **{{legal_name}}**, a Delaware corporation (the "Corporation"), acting pursuant to Section 108 of the Delaware General Corporation Law, hereby takes the following actions by written consent:
1. Appointment of Initial Board of Directors

RESOLVED, that the following person(s) are hereby appointed to serve as the initial director(s) of the Corporation, each to serve until such director's successor is duly elected and qualified or until such director's earlier resignation, removal or death:

RESOLVED, that the initial director(s) are hereby authorized to take all actions necessary or desirable to organize the Corporation and to commence business, including adopting bylaws, electing officers, authorizing the issuance of shares, and taking all other actions contemplated by the Certificate of Incorporation.
This written consent shall be filed with the minutes of the proceedings of the Corporation.

documents.title: Initial Action by Directors (Unanimous Written Consent)

content.text: The undersigned, being all of the directors of **{{legal_name}}**, a Delaware corporation (the "Corporation"), pursuant to Section 141(f) of the Delaware General Corporation Law, hereby take the following actions by unanimous written consent without a meeting:
RESOLVED, that the Certificate of Incorporation of the Corporation, as filed with the Secretary of State of the State of Delaware, is hereby ratified and approved in all respects.
RESOLVED, that the Bylaws attached hereto are hereby adopted as the Bylaws of the Corporation.
RESOLVED, that the following persons are hereby elected to serve as officers of the Corporation, each to serve at the pleasure of the Board of Directors:
RESOLVED, that the Corporation is hereby authorized to issue shares of Common Stock to the following persons in the amounts and for the consideration set forth below:

RESOLVED, that such shares shall be issued pursuant to Restricted Stock Purchase Agreements in the form approved by the Board, and that such issuance is exempt from registration under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, pursuant to Section 4(a)(2) thereof.
RESOLVED, that the Agent Delegation Schedule attached hereto is hereby adopted and incorporated by reference into the Bylaws as provided in Article V thereof.
RESOLVED, that the fiscal year of the Corporation shall end on `{{fiscal_year_end}}`.
RESOLVED, that the officers of the Corporation are hereby authorized to open a bank account in the name of the Corporation and to designate signatories for such account.

RESOLVED, that the officers of the Corporation are hereby authorized to take all actions necessary to qualify the Corporation to do business in any state or jurisdiction where the Corporation's activities require such qualification.
RESOLVED, that the officers of the Corporation are hereby authorized to apply for an Employer Identification Number (EIN) from the Internal Revenue Service.

RESOLVED, that each officer of the Corporation is hereby authorized to take all actions and execute all documents as may be necessary or desirable to carry out the intent and accomplish the purposes of the foregoing resolutions.
This unanimous written consent may be executed in counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original.

documents.title: Articles of Organization
documents.preamble: Filed with the Wyoming Secretary of State. This document establishes the LLC under the Wyoming Limited Liability Company Act.

content.text: The name of the limited liability company is **{{legal_name}}** (the "Company").

The name and address of the registered agent in the State of Wyoming is:

Name: `{{registered_agent_name}}`
Address: `{{registered_agent_address}}`
The Company shall be **manager-managed**. The initial manager is `{{principal_name}}`.
The Company is organized for any lawful purpose permitted under the Wyoming Limited Liability Company Act.
The Company shall have perpetual duration.

The Manager is authorized to designate one or more Agent Operators to manage day-to-day operations of the Company within the scope of the Agent Delegation Schedule adopted by reference in the Operating Agreement. The authority of Agent Operators shall be governed by the Operating Agreement and the Agent Delegation Schedule.

documents.title: Operating Agreement
documents.preamble: Operating Agreement of {{legal_name}}, a Wyoming limited liability company.

content.text: This Operating Agreement (this "Agreement") of **{{legal_name}}** (the "Company"), a Wyoming limited liability company, is entered into effective as of `{{effective_date}}`, by the Members listed in Exhibit A.

**1.1** The Company has been formed as a Wyoming limited liability company by the filing of Articles of Organization with the Wyoming Secretary of State pursuant to the Wyoming Limited Liability Company Act (W.S. § 17-29-101 et seq.) (the "Act").
**1.2** The name of the Company is **{{legal_name}}**.
The Company is formed for any lawful purpose permitted under the Act.
**3.1 Members.** The Members, their capital contributions, and their percentage interests are set forth in Exhibit A.

**3.2 Transfer Restrictions.** No Member may transfer, sell, assign, pledge, or otherwise dispose of all or any part of such Member's interest without the prior written consent of a majority in interest of the other Members.
**4.1 Manager-Managed.** The Company shall be manager-managed. The initial Manager is `{{principal_name}}`.
**4.2 Authority of Manager.** The Manager shall have full authority to manage the business and affairs of the Company, subject to the limitations set forth in this Agreement and the Act.
**5.1 Designation.** The Manager may designate one or more Agent Operators to manage the day-to-day operations of the Company within the scope of authority set forth in the Agent Delegation Schedule.

**5.2 Agent Delegation Schedule.** The Agent Delegation Schedule, as adopted and amended by the Manager with Member approval where required, is incorporated by reference and defines the authority tiers, spending limits, autonomy lanes, approval mechanics, and constraints governing the Agent Operator.

**5.3 Authority Limitations.** The Agent Operator may act only within the scope of authority expressly delegated. The Manager may suspend or revoke Agent authority at any time without notice or cause.

**5.4 Safe Harbor.** No Manager or Member shall be liable for any action taken by an Agent Operator within the scope of delegated authority, provided the Manager acted in good faith in adopting and maintaining the Delegation Schedule. This safe harbor does not apply to fraud, willful misconduct, or gross negligence.
Distributions shall be made at such times and in such amounts as determined by the Manager, in proportion to the Members' percentage interests.
**7.1 Tax Classification.** The Company shall be classified as a partnership (or disregarded entity if single-member) for federal income tax purposes unless the Members elect otherwise.
**7.2 Fiscal Year.** The fiscal year of the Company shall end on `{{fiscal_year_end}}`.
The Company shall indemnify each Manager, Member, and Agent Operator to the fullest extent permitted by the Act.
The Company shall be dissolved upon the occurrence of any event specified in the Act, or upon the written consent of all Members.
This Agreement may be amended only by the written consent of all Members.
This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Wyoming.

The undersigned, being all of the Members of **{{legal_name}}**, a Wyoming limited liability company (the "Company"), hereby take the following actions by written consent without a meeting, pursuant to the Operating Agreement:
RESOLVED, that the Operating Agreement of the Company, in the form attached hereto, is hereby adopted and approved.
RESOLVED, that `{{principal_name}}` is hereby appointed as the Manager of the Company.
RESOLVED, that the Agent Delegation Schedule attached hereto is hereby adopted and incorporated by reference into the Operating Agreement as provided in Section 5 thereof.
RESOLVED, that the fiscal year of the Company shall end on `{{fiscal_year_end}}`.
RESOLVED, that the Manager is authorized to open a bank account in the name of the Company and to designate signatories.
RESOLVED, that the Manager is authorized to apply for an Employer Identification Number from the IRS.
RESOLVED, that the Manager is authorized to take all actions and execute all documents necessary to carry out the foregoing resolutions.

documents.title: Restricted Stock Purchase Agreement
documents.preamble: Agreement between the Company and a purchaser for the purchase of shares subject to vesting and repurchase rights.

content.text: This Restricted Stock Purchase Agreement (this "Agreement") is entered into as of `{{effective_date}}` between **{{legal_name}}**, a Delaware corporation (the "Company"), and `{{purchaser_name}}` (the "Purchaser").

The Company agrees to sell to the Purchaser, and the Purchaser agrees to purchase from the Company, `{{purchase_shares}}` shares of Common Stock at a purchase price of $`{{par_value}}` per share, for a total purchase price of $`{{total_purchase_price}}`, subject to the terms of this Agreement and the Company's governing documents.

The Shares will vest over `{{vesting_months}}` months with a `{{cliff_months}}`-month cliff. If the Purchaser ceases to be a service provider to the Company, the Company will have the right, for the period and on the terms stated in this Agreement, to repurchase any unvested Shares at the original purchase price per share.

items: The Purchaser may not transfer the Shares except as permitted by this Agreement, the Company's governing documents, and applicable securities laws.

The Purchaser acknowledges that it is solely responsible for considering and, if desired, timely filing any election under Section 83(b) of the Internal Revenue Code within the applicable statutory period.

The Purchaser's obligations regarding confidential information, invention assignment, and related proprietary rights will be governed by the separate confidentiality and assignment agreements executed in connection with the Purchaser's service relationship, if any.

content.text: This Agreement will be governed by the laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to conflicts principles. The Company may place legends or stop-transfer instructions on the Shares to reflect restrictions imposed by this Agreement or applicable law.

documents.title: Confidential Information and Inventions Assignment Agreement
documents.preamble: Agreement to protect company confidential information and assign inventions and intellectual property.

content.text: This Confidential Information and Inventions Assignment Agreement (this "Agreement") is entered into as of `{{effective_date}}` by and between **{{legal_name}}** (the "Company") and `{{assignor_name}}` (the "Assignor").

The Assignor will hold all confidential or proprietary information of the Company in strict confidence, will use it solely for the benefit of the Company, and will not disclose it except as authorized in writing by the Company or as required by law.

To the fullest extent permitted by law, the Assignor hereby assigns to the Company all right, title, and interest in and to any and all inventions, works of authorship, developments, software, improvements, processes, know-how, and other intellectual property created, conceived, reduced to practice, authored, or developed by the Assignor alone or with others within the scope of the Assignor's service relationship with the Company or using the Company's confidential information, equipment, supplies, facilities, or trade secret information.

The Assignor will promptly disclose covered inventions to the Company and will execute such additional documents and take such further actions as the Company may reasonably request to confirm, perfect, maintain, or enforce the Company's rights. Any inventions that are excluded from assignment by applicable law or by a properly completed written schedule will remain excluded only to the extent so permitted or identified.

Description of current assignment subject matter: `{{ip_description}}`. This Agreement is governed by the laws of `{{governing_law}}`, without regard to conflicts principles, and may be executed in counterparts and by electronic signature.

documents.title: SAFE Agreement
documents.preamble: Simple Agreement for Future Equity between the Company and an investor.

content.text: This SAFE (Simple Agreement for Future Equity) is entered into as of `{{effective_date}}` by and between **{{entity_legal_name}}** (the "Company") and `{{investor_name}}` (the "Investor"). In exchange for the Investor's payment of `{{purchase_amount}}` (the "Purchase Amount"), the Company issues to the Investor this SAFE on the terms set forth below.
This SAFE is a `{{safe_type}}` SAFE. The valuation cap is `{{valuation_cap}}`. The discount rate is `{{discount_rate}}`. Pro rata participation rights: `{{pro_rata_rights}}`.

Upon the closing of an equity financing, this SAFE will automatically convert into the class or series of capital stock issued in that financing, or into a shadow series intended to preserve the economic rights of this SAFE, at the price determined under the valuation cap, discount, and other mechanics applicable to this SAFE and the definitive financing documents.

If a liquidity event occurs before conversion of this SAFE, the Investor will be entitled to receive, immediately prior to the consummation of the liquidity event, the greater of (a) the Purchase Amount or (b) the amount payable if this SAFE had converted into the number of shares determined under the applicable SAFE mechanics, in each case as reflected in the transaction documents. If a dissolution event occurs before conversion, the Investor will be entitled to receive the Purchase Amount, subject to the rights of creditors and any senior liquidation preferences expressly senior to this SAFE.

items: This SAFE is not debt, bears no interest, and has no maturity date. Until conversion, the Investor has no voting, dividend, or other stockholder rights except as expressly provided in this SAFE or required by law.

The Company represents that it is duly organized, validly existing, and authorized to enter into and perform this SAFE. The Investor represents that it is acquiring this SAFE for investment for its own account and can bear the economic risk of a complete loss.

This SAFE is governed by the laws of `{{governing_law}}`, without regard to conflicts principles. Notices to the Company may be sent to `{{company_notice_address}}`; notices to the Investor may be sent to `{{investor_notice_address}}`. This SAFE may be amended only as permitted by its terms and may be executed in counterparts and by electronic signature.

documents.title: 409A Valuation Report
documents.preamble: Board-use summary of the current 409A valuation and supporting fair market value conclusion.

content.text: This 409A valuation report summarizes the fair market value analysis prepared for **{{entity_legal_name}}** as of `{{effective_date}}` for internal equity pricing, board approval, and recordkeeping purposes.

items: This report is intended to support the Company's good-faith determination of fair market value for option pricing and related equity administration decisions.
The Company should preserve the final valuation report, any engagement materials, management representations, and related board approvals with its permanent records.

If material changes occur in the Company's business, capitalization, financing outlook, or other valuation drivers before the stated expiration date, the Company should consider obtaining an updated valuation.

documents.title: Stock Transfer Agreement
documents.preamble: Agreement documenting a transfer of equity interests between an existing holder and a transferee.

content.text: This Stock Transfer Agreement (this "Agreement") is entered into as of `{{effective_date}}` by and between `{{transferor_name}}` (the "Transferor") and `{{transferee_name}}` (the "Transferee") with respect to equity interests of **{{entity_legal_name}}** (the "Company").

The Transferor agrees to transfer to the Transferee, and the Transferee agrees to acquire from the Transferor, `{{units_transferred}}` units or shares of `{{security_class}}` for aggregate consideration of `{{consideration}}` (`{{price_per_share}}` per share, if applicable), with a closing date of `{{closing_date}}`. The relevant certificate or ledger reference is `{{ledger_reference}}`.

items: The Transferor represents that it has good title to the transferred interests, free and clear of liens created by the Transferor other than restrictions imposed by law or the Company's governing documents, and has authority to enter into this Agreement.
The Transferee represents that it is acquiring the transferred interests for investment for its own account and not with a view to any distribution in violation of applicable securities laws.

Each party acknowledges that this transfer remains subject to the Company's governing documents, any transfer restrictions, rights of first refusal, co-sale rights, or approval requirements, and any applicable securities law compliance requirements.

content.text: The parties will execute such additional instruments and provide such information as may be reasonably requested to complete the transfer, update the stock ledger or capitalization records, and reflect the transfer on the Company's books. The transfer will not be effective on the Company's records until the Company has received evidence reasonably satisfactory to it that all conditions to transfer have been satisfied.

documents.title: Transfer Approval Consent
documents.preamble: Approval resolution for an equity transfer requiring company authorization.

content.text: As of `{{effective_date}}`, the approving authority of **{{entity_legal_name}}** adopts the following resolutions relating to the proposed transfer of equity interests described below.

items: The transfer summarized above is approved, subject to the Company's governing documents, any contractual transfer restrictions, and compliance with applicable securities laws.

The appropriate officers, managers, or other authorized persons are authorized and directed to finalize and execute the related transfer instruments, obtain signatures, and complete such other ministerial acts as they deem necessary or advisable to effect the transfer.

The Company's books, stock ledger, capitalization records, and related records shall be updated promptly after closing, and the executed transfer materials shall be preserved with the Company's permanent records.

documents.title: Board Consent for Financing Transaction
documents.preamble: Approving resolutions for a financing or equity issuance transaction.

content.text: As of `{{effective_date}}`, the governing authority of **{{entity_legal_name}}** approves the financing transaction described below and adopts the following resolutions.

items: The financing transaction described above is approved substantially on the terms presented to the approving authority, together with such non-material revisions as an authorized officer or manager may approve in good faith.

Any appropriate officer, manager, or authorized person is authorized and directed to negotiate, finalize, execute, and deliver the definitive agreements, certificates, notices, and ancillary documents necessary or advisable to consummate the approved transaction.

Following consummation of the transaction, the Company shall update its capitalization records, minute books, and other permanent records to reflect the transaction and preserve the final executed documents and supporting approvals.

documents.title: Equity Issuance Approval
documents.preamble: Approval resolution for issuing equity interests on defined terms.

content.text: As of `{{effective_date}}`, the approving authority of **{{entity_legal_name}}** authorizes the issuance of the securities described below.

items: The securities issuance summarized above is approved on the terms presented, subject to completion of the definitive subscription, purchase, award, or ancillary documents approved by the appropriate authorized person.

The appropriate officers, managers, or other authorized persons are authorized and directed to execute the final documents, collect consideration, issue certificates or electronic book-entry records, and make all related filings, notices, and record updates necessary or advisable to effect the issuance.
The Company shall update its capitalization records and preserve the final executed documents and supporting approval materials with its permanent records promptly following issuance.

documents.title: Subscription Agreement
documents.preamble: Agreement pursuant to which an investor subscribes for company securities.

content.text: This Subscription Agreement (this "Agreement") is entered into as of `{{effective_date}}` by and between **{{entity_legal_name}}** (the "Company") and `{{subscriber_name}}` (the "Subscriber").

The Subscriber hereby subscribes for `{{security_subscribed_for}}` in the amount of `{{quantity_or_principal_amount}}` for aggregate consideration of `{{purchase_amount}}`, to be issued on or about `{{closing_date}}`, subject to the terms of this Agreement and the Company's governing documents.

items: The Subscriber is acquiring the subscribed securities for investment for its own account and not with a view to distribution in violation of applicable securities laws.

The Subscriber has the requisite power and authority to enter into this Agreement and, if applicable, has had an opportunity to ask questions and obtain information regarding the Company and the subscribed securities.
The Subscriber understands that the subscribed securities may be subject to transfer restrictions, legends, and limitations on resale under applicable law and the Company's governing documents.

content.text: Upon receipt of the purchase price and satisfaction of any applicable closing conditions, the Company will issue the subscribed securities, update its capitalization records, and preserve this Agreement and related approvals with its permanent records.

documents.title: Investor Rights Agreement
documents.preamble: Agreement granting an investor specified information, notice, and participation rights.

content.text: This Investor Rights Agreement (this "Agreement") is made as of `{{effective_date}}` by and between **{{entity_legal_name}}** (the "Company") and `{{investor_name}}` (the "Investor").

items: The Company will provide the Investor with periodic financial and operating information customarily provided to similarly situated investors, subject to reasonable confidentiality restrictions.

Upon reasonable advance notice and during normal business hours, the Investor may inspect the Company's books and records to the extent customarily permitted under applicable law and consistent with the Investor's rights under the applicable financing documents.
Subject to the terms of the applicable financing documents, the Investor will have the opportunity to participate pro rata in certain future securities offerings of the Company.

The Company will provide the Investor with notice of material financings, major corporate transactions, and other significant governance events to the extent required by this Agreement or the applicable financing documents.

content.text: The rights granted under this Agreement are subject to confidentiality obligations, customary exceptions, and termination or amendment provisions set forth in the applicable financing documents and this Agreement. This Agreement will be interpreted consistently with the Company's charter, bylaws or operating agreement, investor documents, and applicable law.

documents.title: Consulting Agreement
documents.preamble: Professional services agreement between the Company and a consultant.

content.text: This Consulting Agreement (this "Agreement") is entered into as of `{{effective_date}}` by and between **{{entity_legal_name}}** (the "Company") and `{{consultant_name}}` (the "Consultant").

The Company retains the Consultant to perform the following services: `{{services_description}}`. The Consultant will perform the services in a professional and workmanlike manner consistent with applicable law and generally accepted industry standards.
The engagement will commence on `{{start_date}}` and continue as follows: `{{term_description}}`.

items: The Consultant is an independent contractor and not an employee, partner, joint venturer, fiduciary, or agent of the Company, except as expressly authorized in writing.
The Consultant will hold in confidence and not use or disclose any non-public Company information except as necessary to perform the services or as authorized in writing by the Company.

To the fullest extent permitted by law, all deliverables, reports, software, materials, inventions, and other work product created for the Company within the scope of the services will be owned exclusively by the Company, and the Consultant hereby assigns all right, title, and interest therein to the Company.

content.text: Either party may terminate this Agreement for convenience on `{{termination_notice_days}}` days' prior written notice, and either party may terminate immediately for material breach if the breach is not cured within any applicable notice-and-cure period. Upon termination, the Company will pay the Consultant for services properly performed through the effective date of termination, subject to the terms of this Agreement.
This Agreement is governed by the laws of `{{governing_law}}`, without regard to conflicts of laws principles. This Agreement may be executed in counterparts and by electronic signature.

documents.title: Employment Offer Letter
documents.preamble: Offer letter setting forth the principal terms of employment.

content.text:
**{{effective_date}}**

Dear `{{candidate_name}}`,

**{{entity_legal_name}}** is pleased to offer you employment on the following principal terms.
Your position will be `{{position_title}}`. You will report to `{{reporting_manager}}` and will perform services at `{{work_location}}`, subject to the Company's reasonable business needs.
Your anticipated start date is `{{start_date}}`. Your employment classification is `{{classification}}`, subject to the Company's payroll, tax, and legal compliance determinations.

items: Your employment is contingent on your lawful authorization to work, completion of customary onboarding requirements, and execution of the Company's confidentiality, proprietary information, and policy acknowledgments.

Unless otherwise required by law or set forth in a separate signed written agreement, your employment with the Company will be at will, meaning that either you or the Company may terminate the employment relationship at any time, with or without cause or advance notice.
Any equity award or incentive compensation is subject to approval by the appropriate Company authority and to separate definitive documentation.

content.text: If you wish to accept this offer, please sign and return this letter by `{{offer_expiration_date}}`, unless the Company extends the deadline in writing. This offer is governed by the laws of `{{governing_law}}`, without regard to conflicts principles.
We look forward to working with you.

documents.title: Independent Contractor Services Agreement
documents.preamble: Services agreement for an independent contractor engagement.

content.text: This Independent Contractor Services Agreement (this "Agreement") is entered into as of `{{effective_date}}` by and between **{{entity_legal_name}}** (the "Company") and `{{contractor_name}}` (the "Contractor").
The Contractor will provide the following services: `{{services_description}}`. The engagement will commence on `{{start_date}}` and continue as follows: `{{term_description}}`.

items: The Contractor is an independent contractor and not an employee, agent, partner, or representative of the Company for any purpose, except as expressly authorized in writing.
The Contractor is solely responsible for all compensation, taxes, withholdings, benefits, insurance, and obligations relating to the Contractor and any personnel used by the Contractor.
The Contractor will determine the manner and means of performing the services, subject to the Company's acceptance criteria, timing requirements, and legal compliance requirements.

content.text: The Contractor will hold Company confidential information in confidence and use it only to perform the services. To the fullest extent permitted by law, all deliverables, reports, software, materials, inventions, and other work product created for the Company within the scope of the services will be owned exclusively by the Company, and the Contractor hereby assigns all right, title, and interest therein to the Company.

Either party may terminate this Agreement for convenience on `{{termination_notice_days}}` days' prior written notice, and either party may terminate immediately for material breach if the breach is not cured within any applicable notice-and-cure period. This Agreement is governed by the laws of `{{governing_law}}`, without regard to conflicts principles, and may be executed in counterparts and by electronic signature.

documents.title: Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement
documents.preamble: Agreement governing the exchange and protection of confidential information.

content.text: This Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement (this "Agreement") is entered into as of `{{effective_date}}` by and between **{{entity_legal_name}}** and `{{counterparty_name}}` (each, a "Party" and together, the "Parties").
The Parties wish to evaluate and discuss `{{purpose}}` and, in connection with that purpose, may disclose certain confidential or proprietary information to one another.

Each receiving Party will protect the disclosing Party's confidential information using at least reasonable care, will use it solely for the permitted purpose, and will disclose it only to representatives who have a need to know and who are bound by confidentiality obligations at least as protective as those contained in this Agreement.

Confidential information does not include information that is or becomes publicly available without breach of this Agreement, was lawfully known by the receiving Party without restriction before disclosure, is lawfully received from a third party without breach of any duty, or is independently developed without use of the disclosing Party's confidential information.

Upon written request, each Party will promptly return or destroy the other Party's confidential materials within `{{return_materials_days}}` days, subject to routine backups and legal retention obligations. This Agreement will remain in effect for `{{term_years}}` years, and the confidentiality obligations will survive disclosure for `{{confidentiality_period_years}}` years. Each Party acknowledges that unauthorized use or disclosure may cause irreparable harm for which injunctive relief may be available.

This Agreement does not obligate either Party to proceed with any transaction or relationship. This Agreement is governed by the laws of `{{governing_law}}`, without regard to conflicts principles, and may be executed in counterparts and by electronic signature.
